iterations.strategy: Category A — Plan restructuring. Root cause: data_loading phase (58ms / 117ms total) dominated by MAP_POPULATE forcing synchronous page fault of 913MB across all mmap'd files, including 228MB of orders columns only accessed for ~15K rows. Fix: (1) Remove MAP_POPULATE from ALL mmap calls — for hot runs pages are already in OS page cache, and sequential scan of lineitem naturally triggers hardware prefetch without needing upfront population. (2) Use MADV_SEQUENTIAL for lineitem files (sequential scan), MADV_RANDOM for index/lookup files (sparse random access). (3) Defer mmap of orders and customer columns until after subquery completes — they are only needed for ~15K qualifying rows. (4) Remove the separate data_loading phase entirely — just mmap lazily at point of use. This eliminates the 58ms data_loading overhead; page faults during the scan are near-free on cached pages. Expected total: ~20-30ms.
